focus: [D3-A9/D3] sub-agent/small-model dispatch utilization across the context boundary: out-of-band proof or pre-load-by-construction

graphEvents.label: How can sk-design prove delegated child or small-model utilization across a context boundary?
The design contract requires child dispatch context/proof cards, but current proof_check validates card shape rather than child prompt receipt or parent-verified boundary linkage
Generic Agent I/O evidence is optional/advisory and absent metadata never blocks, so it cannot serve as sk-design's hard boundary gate
cli-opencode already has a design/UI dispatch template that pre-loads sk-design files and proof cards by construction, but benchmark fixtures do not score boundary proof
cli-codex and cli-claude-code checked templates lack the explicit sk-design boundary proof fields present in cli-opencode's design/UI template
skill-benchmark first-failing stages and live evidence omit design boundary proof, child cards, and parent proof_check validation
Delegated design output may be adopted only when route declaration, required design context, child proof cards, and parent-side validation are tied by a boundary proof record

label: The design contract requires child dispatch context/proof cards, but current proof_check validates card shape rather than child prompt receipt or parent-verified boundary linkage
enforcement: ENFORCEABLE for cards/schema/checker; ADVISORY for final design taste

label: Generic Agent I/O evidence is optional/advisory and absent metadata never blocks, so it cannot serve as sk-design's hard boundary gate
enforcement: ENFORCEABLE to detect absence; ADVISORY in the current generic contract

label: cli-opencode already has a design/UI dispatch template that pre-loads sk-design files and proof cards by construction, but benchmark fixtures do not score boundary proof
cli-codex and cli-claude-code checked templates lack the explicit sk-design boundary proof fields present in cli-opencode's design/UI template
enforcement: ENFORCEABLE for template presence; ADVISORY for arbitrary user-written prompts

label: skill-benchmark first-failing stages and live evidence omit design boundary proof, child cards, and parent proof_check validation
Delegated design output may be adopted only when route declaration, required design context, child proof cards, and parent-side validation are tied by a boundary proof record

direction: Treat generic Agent I/O evidence as the hard sk-design boundary gate
reason: The contract explicitly makes the metadata optional/advisory and absence non-blocking; a scoped design boundary proof is needed instead.
